mission.title: Install and Configure Rook Ceph Operator
mission.description: Rook Ceph Operator is a cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes that automates the deployment and management of Ceph storage. It provides self-managing, self-scaling, and self-healing storage services, making it ideal for applications requiring reliable and scalable storage solutions. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to simpler storage solutions.

steps.title: Add Rook Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Rook Helm repository to your local Helm client, allowing you to install Rook from the official source.

```bash

helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Rook Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Rook CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Rook to function properly within your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.3/deploy/examples/crds.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs for Rook.

steps.title: Install Rook Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Rook operator in the 'rook-system' namespace, which manages the lifecycle of Ceph storage.

```bash

helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --create-namespace --version v1.19.3

```

> Installs the Rook Ceph operator.
commands: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --create-namespace --version v1.19.3

steps.title: Create Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Ceph cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD) that Rook manages.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.3/deploy/examples/ceph/cluster.yaml

```

> This command creates a Ceph cluster.

steps.title: Verify Ceph Cluster Status
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Ceph cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system get pod

kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

```

> This command lists all pods in the rook-system namespace to verify their status.
> This command checks the status of the Ceph cluster.
commands: kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rook Ceph Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rook Ceph operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all Ceph data and resources managed by Rook.

```bash

helm uninstall rook-ceph --namespace rook-system

kubectl delete namespace rook-system

```

> Uninstalls the Rook Ceph operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rook Ceph Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and check for any deprecated features. This process includes draining nodes if necessary, upgrading the operator, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

kubectl -n rook-system get pod

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades the Rook Ceph operator.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create ceph cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the required permissions are not set correctly for the operator.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service account used by Rook has the necessary permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen due to misconfiguration in the Ceph cluster settings.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and correct any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Ceph status shows 'HEALTH_WARN'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are issues with the Ceph cluster, such as insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Review the Ceph logs and ensure that all nodes have adequate resources allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no available monitors'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ceph monitors are not running or reachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the monitor pods and ensure they are running correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for CephFS and RBD snapshots, added new monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy storage classes.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.18.x, ensure to migrate any legacy storage classes to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl -n rook-system get pod' showing all pods in 'Running' state and 'ceph status' indicating 'HEALTH_OK'.
codeSnippets: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --create-namespace --version v1.19.3
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources and tools installed.
